EVAL-TRAFFIC-001.risk: Four-way stop rules and driving familiarity vary by region and audience.
EVAL-TRAFFIC-001.plain_language_fallback: The teams need an explicit turn order before work proceeds.
EVAL-TRAFFIC-001.review_note: Use driving frame only when peer right-of-way is familiar.

EVAL-TRAFFIC-002.risk: Peer traffic rules obscure owner authority in incident response.
EVAL-TRAFFIC-002.plain_language_fallback: The incident owner should state the decision path and next action.
EVAL-TRAFFIC-002.review_note: Authority mismatch is the controlling portability risk.

EVAL-TRAFFIC-003.risk: Traffic turn-taking and command language may be blended incorrectly.
EVAL-TRAFFIC-003.plain_language_fallback: Do not blend peer turns with command authority; name the decision owner.
EVAL-TRAFFIC-003.review_note: Require conflict explanation before any metaphor.

EVAL-RISK-001.risk: Color status can fail accessibility, language, and threshold clarity.
EVAL-RISK-001.plain_language_fallback: The status cannot be accepted until the threshold and evidence are named.
EVAL-RISK-001.review_note: Color must never be the only signal.

EVAL-VETO-001.risk: Veto language can imply formal authority or legal process.
EVAL-VETO-001.plain_language_fallback: The launch is blocked until the required approval is cleared or waived.
EVAL-VETO-001.review_note: Use only with explicit requirement authority.

EVAL-VETO-002.risk: Veto language can escalate a preference into a false hard stop.
EVAL-VETO-002.plain_language_fallback: This is a preference or tradeoff; name the decision owner and evidence.

EVAL-VETO-003.risk: Unsupported veto language transfers status and power without evidence.
EVAL-VETO-003.plain_language_fallback: This is an unresolved preference or decision-rights issue.
EVAL-VETO-003.review_note: Suppress frame unless authority and evidence are supplied.

EVAL-ANTI-001.risk: People-as-obstacles framing dehumanizes teams and hides ownership.
EVAL-ANTI-001.plain_language_fallback: The dependency is blocked by unresolved ownership or a decision path.

EVAL-THEME-001.risk: Swimlane metaphor may be unfamiliar and can become slogan theater.
EVAL-THEME-001.safer_alternate: contribution lanes tied to one promise
EVAL-THEME-001.plain_language_fallback: Organize work into a few named contribution lanes tied to one customer promise.
EVAL-THEME-001.review_note: Use only with owner and measure.

EVAL-THEME-002.risk: Three slogans can look like strategy while hiding missing ownership.
EVAL-THEME-002.plain_language_fallback: These are slogans until ownership and measures make them decision lanes.
EVAL-THEME-002.review_note: Hold until themes have operational meaning.

EVAL-STORY-001.risk: Empathy story can erase harm when facts are already known.
EVAL-STORY-001.plain_language_fallback: Facts are now known; name harm, repair, and ownership.
EVAL-STORY-001.review_note: Use story only while facts and intent are unresolved.

EVAL-REL-001.risk: Speed frames can underweight safety in high-stakes customer work.
EVAL-REL-001.plain_language_fallback: Move quickly only inside explicit customer-safety gates.

EVAL-REL-002.risk: Crosswalk duty may be patronizing if mapped to people instead of protected values.
EVAL-REL-002.plain_language_fallback: One side has a duty to slow down to protect the other value.
EVAL-REL-002.review_note: Name the value, not a weak party label.

EVAL-REL-003.risk: Merge and following-distance frames assume driving familiarity and compatible flow.
EVAL-REL-003.plain_language_fallback: Create enough buffer before integrating work into the active system.
EVAL-REL-003.review_note: Use sequence behavior, not a single driving metaphor.

EVAL-REL-004.risk: Unsupported veto language turns unclear power into a false requirement.
EVAL-REL-004.plain_language_fallback: Name the decision owner, tradeoff, and evidence before treating this as blocking.

EVAL-AUD-001.risk: Audience familiarity with driving frames is explicitly unknown.
EVAL-AUD-001.safer_alternate: queue, walking, or direct turn-order explanation
EVAL-AUD-001.plain_language_fallback: Use a queue, walking, or direct turn-order explanation if driving rules are unfamiliar.
EVAL-AUD-001.review_note: Plain language first; metaphor second only if useful.

fixtures.title: Peer teams need turn order
fixtures.target_situation: Two peer teams need turn order around constrained attention.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Check whether parties are peers or one owner has authority.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: The teams need an explicit turn order before work proceeds.

fixtures.title: Owner authority overrides peer turn-taking
fixtures.target_situation: One party owns an incident response decision and must direct the next action.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Confirm who owns incident authority before using a coordination analogy.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: The incident owner should state the decision path and next action.

fixtures.title: Peer turn-taking conflicts with command authority
fixtures.target_situation: Peer turn-taking and incident command both match words in the query.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Decide whether the situation is peer coordination or directed authority.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: Do not blend peer turns with command authority; name the decision owner.

fixtures.title: Green status without threshold
fixtures.target_situation: Status is called green but no threshold is named.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Name the threshold, owner, and evidence that makes the status green.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: The status cannot be accepted until the threshold and evidence are named.

fixtures.title: Required approval is missing
fixtures.target_situation: Launch scores are strong overall, but privacy approval is missing.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Name the requirement, accountable owner, evidence, and clearance or waiver condition.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: The launch is blocked until the required approval is cleared or waived.

fixtures.title: Preference is not a veto
fixtures.target_situation: A stakeholder dislikes a design but no requirement, policy, safety, or customer outcome is blocked.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Check whether the objection is a requirement or a preference.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: This is a preference or tradeoff; name the decision owner and evidence.

fixtures.title: Unsupported veto language
fixtures.target_situation: A senior stakeholder says an option is vetoed but names no requirement, evidence, or accountable owner.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Name the requirement, authority, and evidence before treating it as blocking.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: This is an unresolved preference or decision-rights issue.

fixtures.title: People are not roadblocks
fixtures.target_situation: A query says another team is a roadblock.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Name the blocked dependency, owner, and decision path.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: The dependency is blocked by unresolved ownership or a decision path.

fixtures.title: Three contribution lanes under one promise
fixtures.target_situation: A program leader wants three contribution lanes under one customer promise.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Name the customer promise, lane owners, and measures.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: Organize work into a few named contribution lanes tied to one customer promise.

fixtures.title: Three slogans without ownership
fixtures.target_situation: Three slogans exist but no owner, measure, or customer promise exists.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Check whether each theme has an owner, measure, and customer promise.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: These are slogans until ownership and measures make them decision lanes.

fixtures.title: Empathy story after facts are known
fixtures.target_situation: A bag-of-chips conflict story is used after facts establish harm.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Use uncertainty stories only while intent and facts are unresolved.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: Facts are now known; name harm, repair, and ownership.

fixtures.title: Run Fast needs Run Safe
fixtures.target_situation: Run Fast is suggested for a risky customer migration.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Name the customer-risk checks that make speed acceptable.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: Move quickly only inside explicit customer-safety gates.

fixtures.title: Crosswalk is a boundary for equal turns
fixtures.target_situation: Four-way stop is suggested where one party has protected-party duty.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Name the protected value and duty to slow down.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: One side has a duty to slow down to protect the other value.

fixtures.title: Buffer before merge
fixtures.target_situation: A team wants to merge work into an active system with tight coupling.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Check coupling, reaction time, and approval before joining flow.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: Create enough buffer before integrating work into the active system.

fixtures.title: Unsupported veto falls back to direct language
fixtures.target_situation: Veto Rule is suggested but requirement authority and evidence are missing.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Requirement authority and evidence are missing.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: Name the decision owner, tradeoff, and evidence before treating this as blocking.

fixtures.title: Driving frame may not transfer
fixtures.target_situation: A driving frame is proposed for a non-driver or non-US audience.
fixtures.evidence_boundary: Check whether the audience knows the source scene.
fixtures.plain_language_fallback: Use a queue, walking, or direct turn-order explanation if driving rules are unfamiliar.
